description.en: Build RESTful APIs with Django REST Framework — serializers, ViewSets, routers, and the browsable API.
description.ta: Django REST Framework மூலம் RESTful API-களை உருவாக்குங்கள் — சீரியலைசர்கள், ViewSets, ரூட்டர்கள் மற்றும் உலாவக்கூடிய API.

story_variants.en:
## The Translation Bureau

Vaathiyaar holds up two documents. "Django speaks HTML. APIs speak JSON. **Django REST Framework** (DRF) is the translator that converts Django models into JSON API endpoints automatically."

### Why DRF?
- **Serializers** convert models ↔ JSON
- **ViewSets** provide CRUD with minimal code
- **Routers** auto-generate URL patterns
- **Browsable API** — test in the browser!
- **Authentication** — Token, Session, JWT
- **Permissions** — IsAuthenticated, IsAdminUser, custom

### The DRF Stack

```
Model → Serializer → ViewSet → Router → URL
  ↓         ↓            ↓        ↓       ↓
DB     JSON/Validate  CRUD Logic  URLs   /api/
```

### Key Components

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|--------|
| `Serializer` | Converts model ↔ JSON, validates input |
| `ModelSerializer` | Auto-generates fields from model |
| `ViewSet` | Handles list, create, retrieve, update, delete |
| `Router` | Auto-creates URL patterns from ViewSets |
| `Permission` | Controls who can access what |

> **Key Concept:** DRF follows Django conventions but for APIs. A ModelSerializer is to DRF what a ModelForm is to Django — it auto-generates fields from your model and handles validation.

explanation.en: Install DRF and add 'rest_framework' to INSTALLED_APPS

steps.line:
# blog/models.py
from django.db import models

class Article(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    content = models.TextField()
    author = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    is_published = models.BooleanField(default=False)

explanation.en: A simple Article model — DRF will expose this as an API

steps.line:
# blog/serializers.py
from rest_framework import serializers
from .models import Article

explanation.en: Serializers convert model instances to/from JSON
ModelSerializer auto-generates fields and validation from the model

steps.line:
# blog/views.py
from rest_framework import viewsets, permissions
from .models import Article
from .serializers import ArticleSerializer

explanation.en: ViewSets combine the logic for all CRUD operations

steps.line:
class ArticleViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):
    queryset = Article.objects.all().order_by('-created_at')
    serializer_class = ArticleSerializer
    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticatedOrReadOnly]

explanation.en: ModelViewSet provides list, create, retrieve, update, delete — all in one class!
Override perform_create to auto-set the author from the logged-in user

steps.line:
# blog/urls.py
from django.urls import path, include
from rest_framework.routers import DefaultRouter
from .views import ArticleViewSet

explanation.en: DRF's router auto-generates URL patterns from ViewSets
Register the ViewSet — creates /articles/ and /articles/<pk>/ URLs
Include all auto-generated URLs under /api/ prefix

steps.line:
# Auto-generated endpoints:
# GET    /api/articles/        → List all articles
# POST   /api/articles/        → Create new article
# GET    /api/articles/1/      → Get article #1
# PUT    /api/articles/1/      → Update article #1
# PATCH  /api/articles/1/      → Partial update
# DELETE /api/articles/1/      → Delete article #1

explanation.en: All six CRUD endpoints are auto-generated by the router!

animation_sequence.left_content:
POST /api/articles/
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Token abc123

{"title": "DRF is Amazing", "content": "Building APIs made easy"}
animation_sequence.right_content:
HTTP 201 Created

{"id": 1, "title": "DRF is Amazing", "content": "Building APIs made easy", "author": "pymaster", "created_at": "2026-03-27T10:00:00Z", "is_published": false}

animation_sequence.title: DRF Serialization Pipeline
animation_sequence.code:
from rest_framework import serializers

class ArticleSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    title = serializers.CharField(max_length=200)
    author = serializers.CharField()

data = {'title': 'DRF Guide', 'author': 'Vaathiyaar'}
serializer = ArticleSerializer(data=data)
is_valid = serializer.is_valid()
print(f'Valid: {is_valid}')
print(f'Data: {serializer.validated_data}')

executionSteps.explanation: Define serializer with title and author fields

instruction.en: Build a DRF API for a 'Task' model with fields: title, description, is_completed, and priority (integer). Create a ModelSerializer and a ModelViewSet. Add a custom action that marks a task as completed.
instruction.ta: title, description, is_completed, priority புலங்களுடன் 'Task' மாதிரிக்கான DRF API-ஐ உருவாக்குங்கள்.

en: ModelSerializer auto-generates fields from the model
detail=True means the action requires a specific object (pk)
ta: detail=True என்றால் செயலுக்கு ஒரு குறிப்பிட்ட பொருள் (pk) தேவை

q.en: What does a DRF Serializer do?

en: Converts model instances to/from JSON and validates input
ta: மாதிரி நிகழ்வுகளை JSON-க்கு/JSON-லிருந்து மாற்றுகிறது மற்றும் உள்ளீட்டை சரிபார்க்கிறது

q.en: What does ModelViewSet provide out of the box?

en: Full CRUD: list, create, retrieve, update, delete
ta: முழு CRUD: பட்டியல், உருவாக்கம், மீட்டெடுப்பு, புதுப்பிப்பு, நீக்கம்

q.en: What is the purpose of DRF's DefaultRouter?

en: To auto-generate URL patterns from ViewSets
